description: An aerospace engineer needs to model an internal aerodynamic flow path for a ramjet engine using cross-section lofting. A geometric specification document is located at ~/Desktop/inlet_spec.txt. Read this document to get the required dimensions. Then, in OpenVSP, create a new model containing a single Duct (or Fuselage) component renamed to 'Ramjet_Inlet'. Configure its overall length to 4.5 meters. Modify its cross-sections so the Front Face (Cross-Section 0) is a Rectangle (Width: 0.8 m, Height: 0.4 m) and the Rear Face is a Circle (Diameter: 0.7 m). Save the completed OpenVSP model to ~/Documents/OpenVSP/ramjet_inlet.vsp3. Finally, calculate the theoretical cross-sectional Area Ratio (Rear Area / Front Area) and write a short summary report to ~/Desktop/diffuser_report.txt stating the front area, rear area, and final area ratio.
